definition: Modifying the control law used by a controller to cope with the fact that the parameters of the system being controlled are slowly time-varying or uncertain.
text: Adaptive control [SEP] Modifying the control law used by a controller to cope with the fact that the parameters of the system being controlled are slowly time-varying or uncertain.

definition: Angular gyrus is a brain region involved in integrating information from different sensory modalities to support language and spatial cognition.
text: Angular gyrus [SEP] Angular gyrus is a brain region involved in integrating information from different sensory modalities to support language and spatial cognition.

definition: The anterior insula is a region within the Insular Cortex located at the bottom of the lateral surface of the brain, playing a crucial role in emotional processing; it co-occurs with structures such as the Amygdala and Prefrontal Cortex, and is associated with affective symptoms and emotional responses.
text: Anterior insula [SEP] The anterior insula is a region within the Insular Cortex located at the bottom of the lateral surface of the brain, playing a crucial role in emotional processing; it co-occurs with structures such as the Amygdala and Prefrontal Cortex, and is associated with affective symptoms and emotional responses.

definition: Anterior midline refers to the central axis in the front part of the brain, often involved in cognitive and emotional processing.
text: Anterior midline [SEP] Anterior midline refers to the central axis in the front part of the brain, often involved in cognitive and emotional processing.

definition: The anterior prefrontal cortex is involved in complex cognitive functions including planning and decision-making processes.
text: Anterior prefrontal [SEP] The anterior prefrontal cortex is involved in complex cognitive functions including planning and decision-making processes.

definition: Involved in semantic memory and understanding of language, housing knowledge about concepts and objects.
text: Anterior temporal lobe [SEP] Involved in semantic memory and understanding of language, housing knowledge about concepts and objects.

definition: The attention network emphasizes dorsal attention regions including intraparietal sulcus, dorsal superior parietal lobule, frontal eye fields, superior frontal cortex, temporoparietal junction, ventral frontal cortex, precuneus, and parietal midline. It supports selective attention, spatial orienting, sustained attention, top-down attentional control, and bottom-up reorienting to behaviorally relevant stimuli.
text: Attention network [SEP] The attention network emphasizes dorsal attention regions including intraparietal sulcus, dorsal superior parietal lobule, frontal eye fields, superior frontal cortex, temporoparietal junction, ventral frontal cortex, precuneus, and parietal midline. It supports selective attention, spatial orienting, sustained attention, top-down attentional control, and bottom-up reorienting to behaviorally relevant stimuli.

definition: The auditory network includes primary auditory cortex, planum temporale, planum polare, superior temporal gyrus, superior temporal sulcus, and insula. It supports auditory perception, pitch processing, sound localization, speech-sound processing, music perception, and detection of salient acoustic events.
text: Auditory network [SEP] The auditory network includes primary auditory cortex, planum temporale, planum polare, superior temporal gyrus, superior temporal sulcus, and insula. It supports auditory perception, pitch processing, sound localization, speech-sound processing, music perception, and detection of salient acoustic events.

definition: The ability to identify, interpret, and attach meaning to sound.
text: Auditory perception [SEP] The ability to identify, interpret, and attach meaning to sound.

definition:
Autobiographical memory refers to memory for one's personal history. examples might include memories for experiences that occurred in childhood, the first time learning to drive a car, or even one's social security number or home address.
(from science direct).
text:
Autobiographical memory [SEP] Autobiographical memory refers to memory for one's personal history. examples might include memories for experiences that occurred in childhood, the first time learning to drive a car, or even one's social security number or home address.
(from science direct).

definition: Integration of autonomic nervous system activities to regulate bodily functions during cognitive tasks.
text: Autonomic coordination [SEP] Integration of autonomic nervous system activities to regulate bodily functions during cognitive tasks.

definition: The Basal Ganglia are a group of subcortical structures in the brain that play crucial roles in movement control, habit learning, and cognitive functions; Magnetic Resonance Imaging is often used to visualize these regions, which are found co-occurring in both male and female humans across different age groups including adults.
text: Basal ganglia [SEP] The Basal Ganglia are a group of subcortical structures in the brain that play crucial roles in movement control, habit learning, and cognitive functions; Magnetic Resonance Imaging is often used to visualize these regions, which are found co-occurring in both male and female humans across different age groups including adults.

definition: Process by which sensory input drives attention to new stimuli, overriding current focus.
text: Bottom-up reorienting [SEP] Process by which sensory input drives attention to new stimuli, overriding current focus.

definition: The cerebellum is a region of the brain located at the base near the brainstem, significantly implicated in coordinating voluntary movements, balance, and posture; it co-occurs with humans, males, females, and can be visualized using Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI), with its functions and structures being studied across all age groups including adults.
text: Cerebellum [SEP] The cerebellum is a region of the brain located at the base near the brainstem, significantly implicated in coordinating voluntary movements, balance, and posture; it co-occurs with humans, males, females, and can be visualized using Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI), with its functions and structures being studied across all age groups including adults.

definition: The cingulo-opercular network includes dorsal anterior cingulate cortex, medial superior frontal cortex, anterior insula, frontal operculum, anterior prefrontal cortex, anterior midline regions, and thalamus. It supports salience detection, performance monitoring, error detection, stable task-set maintenance, sustained control, autonomic coordination, and interoception.
text: Cingulo-Opercular network [SEP] The cingulo-opercular network includes dorsal anterior cingulate cortex, medial superior frontal cortex, anterior insula, frontal operculum, anterior prefrontal cortex, anterior midline regions, and thalamus. It supports salience detection, performance monitoring, error detection, stable task-set maintenance, sustained control, autonomic coordination, and interoception.

definition: Cognitive flexibility refers to an individual's ability to adapt their thinking and behavior in response to changing goals or environmental demands, a construct significantly implicated in cognition and executive function, and associated with neural processes within the brain that can be disrupted in various disorders. This term co-occurs with humans, males, females, and is closely linked to cognitive constructs such as cognition and executive function, highlighting its importance in understanding human behavior and neurological health.
text: Cognitive flexibility [SEP] Cognitive flexibility refers to an individual's ability to adapt their thinking and behavior in response to changing goals or environmental demands, a construct significantly implicated in cognition and executive function, and associated with neural processes within the brain that can be disrupted in various disorders. This term co-occurs with humans, males, females, and is closely linked to cognitive constructs such as cognition and executive function, highlighting its importance in understanding human behavior and neurological health.

definition: The deliberate selection of a single alternative amongst multiple alternatives.
text: Decision making [SEP] The deliberate selection of a single alternative amongst multiple alternatives.

definition: The default mode network includes medial prefrontal cortex, posterior cingulate cortex, precuneus, angular gyrus, lateral inferior parietal cortex, lateral temporal cortex, retrosplenial cortex, posterior midline regions, and hippocampal formation. It supports self-referential thought, mind-wandering, spontaneous thought, autobiographical memory, future thinking, social cognition, and theory of mind.
text: Default Mode network [SEP] The default mode network includes medial prefrontal cortex, posterior cingulate cortex, precuneus, angular gyrus, lateral inferior parietal cortex, lateral temporal cortex, retrosplenial cortex, posterior midline regions, and hippocampal formation. It supports self-referential thought, mind-wandering, spontaneous thought, autobiographical memory, future thinking, social cognition, and theory of mind.

definition: Dorsal anterior cingulate cortex is involved in cognitive control, error detection, and response selection during complex problem-solving tasks.
text: Dorsal anterior cingulate cortex (dACC) [SEP] Dorsal anterior cingulate cortex is involved in cognitive control, error detection, and response selection during complex problem-solving tasks.

definition: Dorsal occipital-parietal region involved in spatial cognition and attention, integrating visual and somatosensory information.
text: Dorsal occipital-parietal [SEP] Dorsal occipital-parietal region involved in spatial cognition and attention, integrating visual and somatosensory information.

definition: The Dorsolateral Prefrontal Cortex (DLPFC) is an anatomical region in the brain associated with executive functions such as working memory, reasoning, planning, and regulating social behavior; this area co-occurs with humans across various demographics including females, males, and adults of different ages, and is implicated in cognitive processes relevant to both young adults and older individuals.
text: Dorsolateral prefrontal cortex [SEP] The Dorsolateral Prefrontal Cortex (DLPFC) is an anatomical region in the brain associated with executive functions such as working memory, reasoning, planning, and regulating social behavior; this area co-occurs with humans across various demographics including females, males, and adults of different ages, and is implicated in cognitive processes relevant to both young adults and older individuals.

definition: Processes that identify when an error has been made.
text: Error detection [SEP] Processes that identify when an error has been made.

definition: Executive control manages cognitive processes to guide behavior towards goals, overriding impulses when needed.
text: Executive control [SEP] Executive control manages cognitive processes to guide behavior towards goals, overriding impulses when needed.

definition: The extrastriate visual cortex is a region of the brain involved in visual perception, located outside the striate cortex (primary visual cortex) in the occipital lobe; it processes complex visual information and co-occurs with both broader anatomical regions like the occipital lobe and functional processes such as perception.
text: Extrastriate visual cortex [SEP] The extrastriate visual cortex is a region of the brain involved in visual perception, located outside the striate cortex (primary visual cortex) in the occipital lobe; it processes complex visual information and co-occurs with both broader anatomical regions like the occipital lobe and functional processes such as perception.

definition: Face processing involves neural mechanisms that recognize and interpret facial expressions and identities.
text: Face processing [SEP] Face processing involves neural mechanisms that recognize and interpret facial expressions and identities.

definition: Frontal eye fields are brain areas in the frontal cortex involved in voluntary eye movements and attention.
text: Frontal eye fields [SEP] Frontal eye fields are brain areas in the frontal cortex involved in voluntary eye movements and attention.

definition: Frontal operculum is a region in the frontal cortex involved in speech production and motor skills integration.
text: Frontal operculum [SEP] Frontal operculum is a region in the frontal cortex involved in speech production and motor skills integration.

definition: The frontoparietal control network includes dorsolateral prefrontal cortex, inferior frontal sulcus, lateral prefrontal cortex, anterior prefrontal cortex, inferior parietal lobule, intraparietal sulcus, and lateral temporal cortex. It supports executive control, working memory, cognitive flexibility, task switching, rule and goal representation, decision making, planning, and adaptive control.
text: Frontoparietal Control network [SEP] The frontoparietal control network includes dorsolateral prefrontal cortex, inferior frontal sulcus, lateral prefrontal cortex, anterior prefrontal cortex, inferior parietal lobule, intraparietal sulcus, and lateral temporal cortex. It supports executive control, working memory, cognitive flexibility, task switching, rule and goal representation, decision making, planning, and adaptive control.

definition: Fusiform gyrus is a brain structure in the temporal lobe critical for face and object recognition, as well as semantic memory.
text: Fusiform gyrus [SEP] Fusiform gyrus is a brain structure in the temporal lobe critical for face and object recognition, as well as semantic memory.

definition: Future thinking involves cognitive processes using memory and imagination to envision potential future scenarios and outcomes.
text: Future thinking [SEP] Future thinking involves cognitive processes using memory and imagination to envision potential future scenarios and outcomes.

definition: Hippocampal formation includes structures crucial for memory and spatial navigation, such as hippocampus, dentate gyrus, and subiculum.

definition: Inferior frontal gyrus is a brain region involved in speech production and language processing, part of the left frontal lobe.
text: Inferior frontal gyrus [SEP] Inferior frontal gyrus is a brain region involved in speech production and language processing, part of the left frontal lobe.

definition: The inferior frontal sulcus is a groove on the inner surface of the frontal lobe, co-occurring with the prefrontal cortex, which plays a crucial role in speech production, language processing, and cognitive functions such as working memory and decision-making.
text: Inferior frontal sulcus [SEP] The inferior frontal sulcus is a groove on the inner surface of the frontal lobe, co-occurring with the prefrontal cortex, which plays a crucial role in speech production, language processing, and cognitive functions such as working memory and decision-making.

definition: Inferior parietal lobule processes sensory information to support cognitive functions like language and spatial awareness.

definition: Insula is a cerebral cortex region involved in consciousness and emotion regulation, integrating sensory, motor, and cognitive information.

definition: Perception of stimuli inside the body, e.g. hunger, thirst, pain.

definition: Area of the brain involved in processing visual information and spatial awareness within the parietal lobe.

definition: The language network includes inferior frontal gyrus, posterior superior temporal gyrus, middle temporal gyrus, anterior temporal lobe, angular gyrus, and supramarginal gyrus. It supports speech comprehension and production, syntax processing, semantic processing, reading, lexical access, phonological processing, and sentence-level language integration.
text: Language network [SEP] The language network includes inferior frontal gyrus, posterior superior temporal gyrus, middle temporal gyrus, anterior temporal lobe, angular gyrus, and supramarginal gyrus. It supports speech comprehension and production, syntax processing, semantic processing, reading, lexical access, phonological processing, and sentence-level language integration.

definition: Lateral inferior parietal cortex processes spatial awareness and attention, integrating sensory information for cognitive tasks.

definition: Lateral occipital cortex processes visual information and is crucial for object recognition and scene perception.

definition: Lateral PFC is a brain region crucial for executive functions, working memory, and cognitive control.

definition: Lateral temporal cortex processes and stores semantic knowledge, aiding in language comprehension and object recognition.

definition: Region involved in visual word recognition and linking seen words to meaning.

definition: The process by which contact is made with the lexicon on the basis of an initial acoustic-phonetic or phonological representation of some portion of the speech input, the result of lexical success is a cohort of potential word candidates which are compatible with this initial analysis.
text: Lexical access [SEP] The process by which contact is made with the lexicon on the basis of an initial acoustic-phonetic or phonological representation of some portion of the speech input, the result of lexical success is a cohort of potential word candidates which are compatible with this initial analysis.

definition: The medial prefrontal cortex (mPFC) is an anatomical region within the prefrontal cortex that plays a crucial role in emotional regulation, decision-making, and social cognition; it co-occurs with areas such as the orbitofrontal cortex, amygdala, and is part of the Default Mode Network (DMN), and has been implicated in conditions like schizophrenia.
text: Medial prefrontal cortex (mPFC) [SEP] The medial prefrontal cortex (mPFC) is an anatomical region within the prefrontal cortex that plays a crucial role in emotional regulation, decision-making, and social cognition; it co-occurs with areas such as the orbitofrontal cortex, amygdala, and is part of the Default Mode Network (DMN), and has been implicated in conditions like schizophrenia.

definition: Medial superior frontal cortex is a brain region involved in higher-order cognitive functions and executive control processes.

definition: Middle temporal gyrus is a brain region involved in processing visual motion and integrating it with other sensory information.

definition: Middle temporal visual area processes motion perception and object recognition in the brain.

definition: Mind-wandering involves spontaneous thoughts that deviate from the current environment or task focus.

definition: Motion Perception is a cognitive construct involved in the brain's ability to interpret visual information, enabling humans (including males and females) to perceive and respond to moving objects. This process is crucial for navigation, object recognition, and overall spatial awareness, as evidenced through brain mapping studies in adults.
text: Motion perception [SEP] Motion Perception is a cognitive construct involved in the brain's ability to interpret visual information, enabling humans (including males and females) to perceive and respond to moving objects. This process is crucial for navigation, object recognition, and overall spatial awareness, as evidenced through brain mapping studies in adults.

definition: The motor or somatomotor network includes primary motor cortex, primary somatosensory cortex, premotor cortex, supplementary motor area, paracentral lobule, cerebellum, basal ganglia, and thalamus. It supports voluntary movement execution, motor planning, touch, pain, temperature, proprioception, sensorimotor integration, motor learning, and motor coordination.
text: Motor network [SEP] The motor or somatomotor network includes primary motor cortex, primary somatosensory cortex, premotor cortex, supplementary motor area, paracentral lobule, cerebellum, basal ganglia, and thalamus. It supports voluntary movement execution, motor planning, touch, pain, temperature, proprioception, sensorimotor integration, motor learning, and motor coordination.

definition: Motor coordination involves precise timing and control of muscle movements to perform skilled actions smoothly.
text: Motor coordination [SEP] Motor coordination involves precise timing and control of muscle movements to perform skilled actions smoothly.

definition: The process of improving motor skills, the smoothness and accuracy of movements.
text: Motor learning [SEP] The process of improving motor skills, the smoothness and accuracy of movements.

definition: The conception of a future motoric action.

definition: The conscious experience of discriminate music among all types of sensory stimuli.

definition: Is the ability to perceive an objectâs physical properties (such as shape, colour and texture) and apply semantic attributes to the object, which includes the understanding of its use, previous experience with the object and how it relates to others.
text: Object recognition [SEP] Is the ability to perceive an objectâs physical properties (such as shape, colour and texture) and apply semantic attributes to the object, which includes the understanding of its use, previous experience with the object and how it relates to others.

definition: An unpleasant sensory and emotional experience associated with actual or potential tissue damage.

definition: Paracentral lobule is a part of the brain located in the medial frontal cortex involved in processing somatosensory and motor information.

definition: Parietal midline refers to the central region of the parietal lobe involved in spatial awareness and attention.

definition: The online evaluation of one's performance on a task.

definition: Refers to the use of phonological information (i.e., the sound of one's language) in processing written and oral language.

definition: Pitch processing involves neural mechanisms that detect and interpret the frequency of sound waves to perceive musical notes and tones.

definition: Formulation, evaluation and selection of a sequence of actions to achieve a desired goal.

definition: Planum polare is a region in the temporal cortex involved in processing aspects of language and semantics.

definition: The planum temporale is a region of gray matter located in the temporal lobe, specifically within the superior temporal gyrus, which plays a crucial role in speech, language, auditory processing, and perception; it often shows asymmetry between the left and right hemispheres and has been associated with conditions such as schizophrenia.
text: Planum temporale [SEP] The planum temporale is a region of gray matter located in the temporal lobe, specifically within the superior temporal gyrus, which plays a crucial role in speech, language, auditory processing, and perception; it often shows asymmetry between the left and right hemispheres and has been associated with conditions such as schizophrenia.

definition: Posterior cingulate cortex is a brain region involved in memory, emotion, and self-related processes.

definition: Posterior midline regions are brain areas near the back and middle of the head, involved in cognitive functions like memory and attention.

definition: Posterior superior temporal gyrus is a brain region involved in auditory processing and language comprehension.

definition: Precuneus is a brain region involved in self-reflection and memory recall located in the parietal lobe.

definition: Premotor cortex is a brain area involved in planning and executing movement, situated anterior to the primary motor cortex.
text: Premotor cortex [SEP] Premotor cortex is a brain area involved in planning and executing movement, situated anterior to the primary motor cortex.

definition: Primary area in the brain's temporal lobe responsible for processing basic sound information and initiating auditory perception.
text: Primary auditory cortex [SEP] Primary area in the brain's temporal lobe responsible for processing basic sound information and initiating auditory perception.

definition: Primary motor cortex is the brain area responsible for planning and executing voluntary movement commands.

definition: Primary area in the parietal lobe processing sensory information from the body, enabling touch and proprioception awareness.

definition: The Primary Visual Cortex, also known as V1 or striate cortex, is a specialized region in the occipital lobe of the human brain that plays a crucial role in processing visual information; it receives input from the lateral geniculate nucleus of the thalamus and integrates this data to form basic visual perceptions.
text: Primary visual cortex [SEP] The Primary Visual Cortex, also known as V1 or striate cortex, is a specialized region in the occipital lobe of the human brain that plays a crucial role in processing visual information; it receives input from the lateral geniculate nucleus of the thalamus and integrates this data to form basic visual perceptions.

definition: The sense of the relative position of neighboring parts of the body.

definition: Decoding symbols to derive their meaning.

definition: Region in the brain located in the medial parietal cortex involved in spatial memory and navigation.

definition: Neural patterns encoding rules or objectives guiding behavior and decision-making processes.

definition: Salience detection identifies stimuli important for behavior by enhancing relevant neural signals over others.

definition: Scene processing involves neural mechanisms that integrate visual information to recognize and interpret complex environments.
text: Scene processing [SEP] Scene processing involves neural mechanisms that integrate visual information to recognize and interpret complex environments.

definition: When multiple external sensory inputs are present, the process of dedicating cognitive and perceptual resources to one type/set of input and attenuating receptiveness to other inputs.
text: Selective attention [SEP] When multiple external sensory inputs are present, the process of dedicating cognitive and perceptual resources to one type/set of input and attenuating receptiveness to other inputs.

definition: Self-referential thought involves cognitive processes that focus on the self, including introspection and evaluation of one's own thoughts and actions.
text: Self-referential thought [SEP] Self-referential thought involves cognitive processes that focus on the self, including introspection and evaluation of one's own thoughts and actions.

definition: The cognitive processing of extracting meaning from any form of language (e.g. human language, programming language) as well as formal logics and semiotics.
text: Semantic processing [SEP] The cognitive processing of extracting meaning from any form of language (e.g. human language, programming language) as well as formal logics and semiotics.

definition: Process linking sensory input with motor output to produce coordinated behavior and learning from experience.
text: Sensorimotor integration [SEP] Process linking sensory input with motor output to produce coordinated behavior and learning from experience.

definition: The encoding, storage, retrieval, and processing, in the brain, of information relating to conspecifics, or members of the same species.
text: Social cognition [SEP] The encoding, storage, retrieval, and processing, in the brain, of information relating to conspecifics, or members of the same species.

definition: Somatosensory processing involves the brain's interpretation of touch, temperature, pain, and proprioception from body parts.
text: Somatosensory processing [SEP] Somatosensory processing involves the brain's interpretation of touch, temperature, pain, and proprioception from body parts.

definition: Sound localization refers to the cognitive construct by which humans perceive the direction from which a sound originates, involving complex brain processes that are studied through techniques such as acoustic stimulation and brain mapping; this ability is present in both males and females and is crucial for spatial awareness and navigation.
text: Sound localization [SEP] Sound localization refers to the cognitive construct by which humans perceive the direction from which a sound originates, involving complex brain processes that are studied through techniques such as acoustic stimulation and brain mapping; this ability is present in both males and females and is crucial for spatial awareness and navigation.

definition: Spatial orienting directs attention to a specific location in space, preparing neural circuits to process stimuli from that area.
text: Spatial orienting [SEP] Spatial orienting directs attention to a specific location in space, preparing neural circuits to process stimuli from that area.

definition: Speech comprehension refers to the cognitive process of understanding spoken language, which involves integrating auditory input from the Auditory Cortex, processing temporal patterns relevant to speech in the Temporal Lobe, and integrating this information with existing linguistic knowledge stored in areas such as the Prefrontal Cortex for meaningful interpretation. This construct co-occurs with speech perception, language processing, and is significantly related to the functioning of both auditory and higher-order cognitive regions.
text: Speech comprehension [SEP] Speech comprehension refers to the cognitive process of understanding spoken language, which involves integrating auditory input from the Auditory Cortex, processing temporal patterns relevant to speech in the Temporal Lobe, and integrating this information with existing linguistic knowledge stored in areas such as the Prefrontal Cortex for meaningful interpretation. This construct co-occurs with speech perception, language processing, and is significantly related to the functioning of both auditory and higher-order cognitive regions.

definition: Is the process by which spoken words are selected to be produced, have their phonetics formulated and then finally are articulated by the motor system in the vocal apparatus. speech production can be spontaneous such as when a person creates the words of a conversation, reaction such as when they name a picture or read aloud a written word, or a vocal imitation such as in speech repetition.  speech production is not the same as language production since language can also be produced manually by signs.
text: Speech production [SEP] Is the process by which spoken words are selected to be produced, have their phonetics formulated and then finally are articulated by the motor system in the vocal apparatus. speech production can be spontaneous such as when a person creates the words of a conversation, reaction such as when they name a picture or read aloud a written word, or a vocal imitation such as in speech repetition.  speech production is not the same as language production since language can also be produced manually by signs.

definition: Processing of auditory signals to recognize and discriminate speech sounds.
text: Speech-sound processing [SEP] Processing of auditory signals to recognize and discriminate speech sounds.

definition: Spontaneous thought refers to internally generated thinking that occurs without direct external stimulation or explicit goal-directed focus.

definition: Maintaining cognitive readiness to execute a specific set of tasks over time.

definition: Area of the frontal lobe involved in higher cognitive functions such as planning and decision making.

definition: Superior temporal gyrus is a brain region involved in processing auditory information and language comprehension.

definition: The superior temporal sulcus is a groove on the surface of the brain located in the temporal lobe, which plays a crucial role in perception and processing of visual motion and social cues; it co-occurs with the temporal lobe, temporoparietal region, and visual cortex, and interacts with areas such as the prefrontal cortex, amygdala, and posterior superior temporal sulcus.
text: Superior temporal sulcus [SEP] The superior temporal sulcus is a groove on the surface of the brain located in the temporal lobe, which plays a crucial role in perception and processing of visual motion and social cues; it co-occurs with the temporal lobe, temporoparietal region, and visual cortex, and interacts with areas such as the prefrontal cortex, amygdala, and posterior superior temporal sulcus.

definition: Supplementary motor area plans and coordinates complex movements and is involved in processing spatial relationships.

definition: Part of the inferior parietal lobule involved in language processing and social cognition, aiding semantic retrieval.

definition: The ability to maintain a consistent behavioral response during continuous and repetitive activity.

definition: Sustained control involves maintaining attention and effort over prolonged periods to achieve complex cognitive tasks.
text: Sustained control [SEP] Sustained control involves maintaining attention and effort over prolonged periods to achieve complex cognitive tasks.

definition: Syntax processing involves neural mechanisms decoding grammar and sentence structure in language comprehension.
text: Syntax processing [SEP] Syntax processing involves neural mechanisms decoding grammar and sentence structure in language comprehension.

definition: The process of switching from one task or goal to another, depending on the context or instructions, that is, the process of switching between task sets.

definition: Temperature refers to the measure of thermal energy in biological systems, playing a crucial role in various processes such as thermosensing and brain function, and is implicated in conditions like Escherichia coli infections; it is also used as a parameter in advanced imaging techniques including Magnetic Resonance Imaging and Magnetic Resonance Spectroscopy.
text: Temperature [SEP] Temperature refers to the measure of thermal energy in biological systems, playing a crucial role in various processes such as thermosensing and brain function, and is implicated in conditions like Escherichia coli infections; it is also used as a parameter in advanced imaging techniques including Magnetic Resonance Imaging and Magnetic Resonance Spectroscopy.

definition: The temporoparietal junction (TPJ) is an anatomical region located at the meeting point of the temporal and parietal lobes in the brain, significant for cognitive functions such as attention, perception, and theory of mind; it co-occurs with areas like the medial prefrontal cortex and insular cortex, and is involved in processes including cognition.
text: Temporoparietal junction [SEP] The temporoparietal junction (TPJ) is an anatomical region located at the meeting point of the temporal and parietal lobes in the brain, significant for cognitive functions such as attention, perception, and theory of mind; it co-occurs with areas like the medial prefrontal cortex and insular cortex, and is involved in processes including cognition.

definition: The thalamus is a paired structure within the brain that acts as a relay center for nearly all sensory information (except olfactory) before it reaches the cerebral cortex, significantly implicated in Humans, Male, and Female, and often visualized through Magnetic Resonance Imaging, particularly in Adult subjects.
text: Thalamus [SEP] The thalamus is a paired structure within the brain that acts as a relay center for nearly all sensory information (except olfactory) before it reaches the cerebral cortex, significantly implicated in Humans, Male, and Female, and often visualized through Magnetic Resonance Imaging, particularly in Adult subjects.

definition: The ability for a person to connect emotional states to themselves and others and understand that other people may have different  beliefs, desires, or intentions from one's self.  it is intimately connected with the development of a person's ability to analyze and interpret the intentions of others.
text: Theory of mind [SEP] The ability for a person to connect emotional states to themselves and others and understand that other people may have different  beliefs, desires, or intentions from one's self.  it is intimately connected with the development of a person's ability to analyze and interpret the intentions of others.

definition: Top-down attentional control refers to the cognitive process by which higher-level cognitive functions, such as working memory and goal-directed thinking, influence the allocation of attentional resources. This mechanism is crucial for filtering relevant information from the environment and modulating sensory processing based on current goals and expectations, co-occurring with concepts like attentional control in biomedical research.
text: Top-down attentional control [SEP] Top-down attentional control refers to the cognitive process by which higher-level cognitive functions, such as working memory and goal-directed thinking, influence the allocation of attentional resources. This mechanism is crucial for filtering relevant information from the environment and modulating sensory processing based on current goals and expectations, co-occurring with concepts like attentional control in biomedical research.

definition: Touch, in the context of cognitive constructs, refers to the sensory perception mediated by somatosensory receptors and plays a crucial role in human interaction, development, and emotional processing; it is implicated across genders (Male, Female) and age groups (Adults), significantly influencing brain mapping and cognitive functions.
text: Touch [SEP] Touch, in the context of cognitive constructs, refers to the sensory perception mediated by somatosensory receptors and plays a crucial role in human interaction, development, and emotional processing; it is implicated across genders (Male, Female) and age groups (Adults), significantly influencing brain mapping and cognitive functions.

definition: Ventral frontal cortex is involved in cognitive control and regulation of behavior, speech production, and aspects of language comprehension.
text: Ventral frontal cortex [SEP] Ventral frontal cortex is involved in cognitive control and regulation of behavior, speech production, and aspects of language comprehension.

definition: The ventral occipitotemporal cortex is a region of the brain located in the temporal lobe and occipital lobe, playing a crucial role in object recognition and visual word processing; it co-occurs with both the temporal lobe and occipitotemporal cortex.
text: Ventral occipitotemporal cortex [SEP] The ventral occipitotemporal cortex is a region of the brain located in the temporal lobe and occipital lobe, playing a crucial role in object recognition and visual word processing; it co-occurs with both the temporal lobe and occipitotemporal cortex.

definition: The visual network includes primary and extrastriate visual cortices, lateral occipital cortex, fusiform gyrus, ventral occipitotemporal cortex, middle temporal visual area, lateral temporal-occipital cortex, intraparietal visual regions, and dorsal occipital-parietal areas. It supports visual perception, object and face recognition, scene processing, motion perception, visuospatial processing, and visually guided attention.
text: Visual network [SEP] The visual network includes primary and extrastriate visual cortices, lateral occipital cortex, fusiform gyrus, ventral occipitotemporal cortex, middle temporal visual area, lateral temporal-occipital cortex, intraparietal visual regions, and dorsal occipital-parietal areas. It supports visual perception, object and face recognition, scene processing, motion perception, visuospatial processing, and visually guided attention.

definition: Two-stage process in which attention is distributed uniformly over the external visual scene and processing of information is performed in parallel, attention is then concentrated to a specific area of the visual scene (i.e. it is focused), and processing is performed in a serial fashion.
text: Visual attention [SEP] Two-stage process in which attention is distributed uniformly over the external visual scene and processing of information is performed in parallel, attention is then concentrated to a specific area of the visual scene (i.e. it is focused), and processing is performed in a serial fashion.

definition: Visual perception is the ability to interpret the surrounding environment by processing information that is contained in visible light. the resulting perception is also known as eyesight, sight, or vision.
text: Visual perception [SEP] Visual perception is the ability to interpret the surrounding environment by processing information that is contained in visible light. the resulting perception is also known as eyesight, sight, or vision.

definition: Processing and interpreting visual information to understand spatial relationships and navigate the environment.
text: Visuospatial processing [SEP] Processing and interpreting visual information to understand spatial relationships and navigate the environment.

definition: Process by which the brain plans and initiates conscious actions through neural pathways involving motor cortex and basal ganglia.
text: Voluntary movement execution [SEP] Process by which the brain plans and initiates conscious actions through neural pathways involving motor cortex and basal ganglia.

definition: Active maintenance and flexible updating of goal/task relevant information (items, goals, strategies, etc.) in a form that resists interference but has limited capacity. these representations may involve flexible binding of representations, may be characterized by the absence of external support for the internally maintained representations, and are frequently temporary due to ongoing interference.
text: Working memory [SEP] Active maintenance and flexible updating of goal/task relevant information (items, goals, strategies, etc.) in a form that resists interference but has limited capacity. these representations may involve flexible binding of representations, may be characterized by the absence of external support for the internally maintained representations, and are frequently temporary due to ongoing interference.
